provenance.trust: docs-sourced from designsystem.gov.ae — provisional, needs revalidation against the live page before each release; TDRA updates the checklist with design-system releases

sections: SECTION 1 : STRATEGY, USER EXPERIENCE AND CONTENT

items.section: SECTION 1 : STRATEGY, USER EXPERIENCE AND CONTENT
items.question: Have you implemented a version of the design system greater than 1.0?

items.section: SECTION 1 : STRATEGY, USER EXPERIENCE AND CONTENT
items.question: Have you created a target audience Empathy Map? Refer to Insights on designsystem.gov.ae/insights/creating-an-empathy-map-the-first-step-in-design-thinking

items.section: SECTION 1 : STRATEGY, USER EXPERIENCE AND CONTENT
items.question: Did you conduct internal workshop to gather requirements and document stakeholder requirements?

items.section: SECTION 1 : STRATEGY, USER EXPERIENCE AND CONTENT
items.question: Have you analysed the analytics of the current website to understand has been successful and what is not?

items.section: SECTION 1 : STRATEGY, USER EXPERIENCE AND CONTENT
items.question: Have you created the sitemap and information architecture for the website?

items.section: SECTION 1 : STRATEGY, USER EXPERIENCE AND CONTENT
items.question: Have you conducted user research before planning the design and content?

items.section: SECTION 1 : STRATEGY, USER EXPERIENCE AND CONTENT
items.question: Have you created (atleast) 4 user persona's in your ethnographic research?

items.section: SECTION 1 : STRATEGY, USER EXPERIENCE AND CONTENT
items.question: Have you created a user flow journey for the persona's?

items.section: SECTION 1 : STRATEGY, USER EXPERIENCE AND CONTENT
items.question: Have you created low-fidelity wireframes to map the user journey?

items.section: SECTION 1 : STRATEGY, USER EXPERIENCE AND CONTENT
items.question: Have you created a content strategy with an Arabic-first approach?

items.section: SECTION 1 : STRATEGY, USER EXPERIENCE AND CONTENT
items.question: Have you added "real copy" to your wireframes?

items.section: SECTION 1 : STRATEGY, USER EXPERIENCE AND CONTENT
items.question: Have you created a map of static and dynamic content as per the information architecture?

items.section: SECTION 1 : STRATEGY, USER EXPERIENCE AND CONTENT
items.question: Have you created a strategy and plan for content regarding Text, Images and Video?

items.section: SECTION 1 : STRATEGY, USER EXPERIENCE AND CONTENT
items.question: Have you read and understood the tone, voice, and structure of content mentioned on the Content Guideline? Refer to designsystem.gov.ae/guidelines/content

items.section: SECTION 1 : STRATEGY, USER EXPERIENCE AND CONTENT
items.question: Have you created copy in English following the Oxford Dictionary and using British English as the standard?

items.section: SECTION 1 : STRATEGY, USER EXPERIENCE AND CONTENT
items.question: Have you created copy in Arabic following the MSA dialect
Has the design team studied the Typography guideline? Refer to designsystem.gov.ae/guidelines/typography
Has your design process included the approved font mentioned in the design system?
Has the design process added the the approved fonts for headings in the mentioned font weight?
Has the design process planned for responsive typography?
Has the design process ensured spacing between sections as requited by WCAG 2.1 and mentioned in the guideline?
Has the design team adhered to the neutral colour palette for background colour?
Has the design team adhered to the primary text colour of AEBLACK-800?
Has the design team adhered to the use of the primary colour of AEGOLD-600 for actions?
Have you tested sections of your website's background and foreground colour usage to be 3:1?
Have you tested contrast ratio of action elements background and foreground to be 4.5:1?
Are functional colours such as errors, warnings, success and information being followed as described?
FOR MINISTRIES ONLY: Are you following the primary colour palette only using AEGOLD and AEBLACK as your primary colours?
Has the design process ensured the distinction between primary and secondary colour use for action buttons
Has the design team studied the Imagery guideline? Refer to designsystem.gov.ae/guidelines/imagery
Are you using simple backgrounds for your hero image? Or have you created the hero imagery strategy to include graphics using the primary and secondary palette?
Have you used photography based images adhering to principles mentioned in the Imagery Guideline?
Are you following the use of images as per the brand guidelines of the United Arab Emirates Federal Government?
Is the design of custom images following the principles of how to place text on images?
If you are using illustrations, are they following the principles of creating them? Answer Completed, if you are not using any illustrations.
Has the design team studied the Iconography guideline? Refer to designsystem.gov.ae/guidelines/iconography
Are you using the approved library for use of icons?
If you have designed custom icons, are you following the instructions of how to place icons centrally as described in the Figma file?
Has the design process ensured that all icons are a minimum of 24px in width and height?
Are you following the principles of where to place icons as mentioned in the Iconography guideline?
Has the design team studied the Layout and Spacing guideline? Refer to designsystem.gov.ae/guidelines/layout
Has the design process created layouts and wireframes at 1536px as the canvas and 1480px as the container?
Has the design process created mockups of the design for mobile?
Has the design process followed a mobile-first approach?
Has the design process ensured the distinction between primary and secondary colour use for action buttons
Has the design process ensured the use of the spacing grid as described in the guideline?
Is there a clear clear and visual difference between the design styles applied to action buttons and hyperlinks?
Has the design process ensured the right use of components as described in the Action guidelines of the design system? Refer to designsystem.gov.ae/guidelines/actions
Has the design team created form elements using the approved components of the design system?
Has the design team tested the design for visual accessibility?
Has the design team created hidden design elements such as "Skip to content"
Has the design team created images and graphics that follow the Imagery guideline for accessibility?
Has the design team ensured that colour contrast is maintained in graphics and images as well?
Has the design team tested the layout when zoomed in to a browser at 175%?
Is the design for the website following approved template layouts?
Is the design for the website using the approved header and footer elements without change?
Is the overall design utilising blocks, cards, and components as described in the design system?
Have you created user-friendly error pages for 404, 403, 500
Has the design team created a prototype and tested the design against the user journey?
Was user-centric design testing conducted - as described on designsystem.gov.ae/resources/assets-and-tools
Were consistency checks carried out validating the design with guidelines and components?
Have you installed the latest version of the design system via NPM?
Have you built your frontend using TailwindCSS classes?
Have you ensured each part of your website's sections are divided into reusable blocks or components?
Is your frontend code structurally hierarchical using the accurate placement of <h1> to <h6> tags?
Are you separating sections of your website into separate containers and not boxing your entire website?
Are you using components and block code as mentioned in the design system?
For parts of your websites that are not components and blocks, are you adhering to WAI accessibility principles?
Have you added the "aria-hidden="true" attribute to icons that must not be read by screen readers?
Have you ensured all icons have a supporting text - either visible or wrapped in the "sr-only" class?
Are you adding accurate aria attributes to form elements and labels?
Have you ensured progressive enhacements to accessibility when using navigation elements?
Have you tested your frontend code to be AA Level compliant with WCAG 2.1
Can you navigate your entire website using only a keyboard - without the use of a trackpad or a mouse?
Have you added a focus state to every action element? Have you tested your website for focus states?
Have you carried out Mobile responsiveness testing?
Have you tested your frontend code on Edge, Chrome, Firefox, Safari, Safari mobile, Chrome mobile?
Have you carried out design validation testing to ensure that your frontend matches the design?
Have you used any testing tool listed on designsystem.gov.ae/resources/assets-and-tools
Are you using the approved header layout and design as mentioned on designsystem.gov.ae/docs/blocks/header
Have you used the header code as described for mobile use?
Are you using the approved footer layout and design as mentioned on designsystem.gov.ae/docs/blocks/footer
Have you used the footer code as described for mobile use?
Is your hero block using <picture> with srcset to add a different image for mobile and desktop?
Have you used the login block as described with UAE Pass as the primary login method?
Have you added the Page Rating block to all service card pages?
Is the Doctype HTML5 on the top of all pages?
Is the charset declared as UTF-8?
Is the viewport declared accurately?
Are all meta title and description tags unique? Have you ensured unique dynamic content when required?
Has a favicon been included with variations for bookmarking sizes?
Are you using the Apple-meta tags to control the colour of your website's pre header?
Are you using "rel=canonical" to avoid duplicate content page indexing?
Have you ensured adding the "direction" attribute to your body tag according to your language?
Have you ensured added the "lang" attribute to your HTML tag based on your language?
Have you ensured all pages list the alternate language tag?
Have you ensured you have the facility to generate open meta tags for social media?
Have you structured your HTML using HTML5 semantic tags (such as <header>, <footer>, <section>, <main>)
Have you created the Error pages - 404, 403, 500
Have you ensured you are following the design system guideline on use of hyperlinks to add "rel=noopener" where required?
Are all pages and all HTML code W3C compliant?
Have you ensured the use of Google Fonts and not loading fonts from your server?
Have you tested all pages on responsive breakpoints mentioned in the design system?
Have you ensured that CSS is NON-Blocking?
Are you implementing graceful degradation for your website on outdated browsers?
Are you informing the user if they are using an outdated browser as described in the design system's browser support?
Are you minifying your rendered CSS and JavaScript for a production environment?
Are you implementing a cache system for faster page load?
Are all images dynamically adding an alt tag that is meaningful to the image?
Are you using Picture/Srcset for images as described in the design system?
Have you enabled lazy loading for images?
Are you using WebP as your image format primarily?
Are you videos being loaded based on user's device and internet speed? This would happen by default if you are hosting public videos on Youtube or Vimeo
Eliminating all images on the page, is your page weight below 500KB while loading?
With all images on your page, is your page weight below 4MB while loading?
Have you consulted with the backend team on preconnection time, dns handshake time and how it is affecting the loading speed of your website?
Have you consulted with the backend team on WebMetric Measurement Criteria to measure LCP and FCP?
Have you ensured JavaScript is placed at the end of your website?
Have you taken into account third party scripts and software that will affect your load time in a production environment?
Have you added a cookie banner?
Are you using a Headless CMS?
Does your technology strategy involve the use of a JavaScript Framework (Such as Next.js) with a headless CMS for content management?
Does your content management system include the ability to manage Meta Tags as a page level?
Are you ensuring the correct using of image sizes added as part of your content for each page that will be used by open tags?
Does your content management system generate a Sitemap.xml file?
